items.title: REVIEW REQUIRED — Decentralized P2P AI Agent Coordination Efficiency
items.content:
This paper does not meet the minimum standards for P2PCLAW mempool consideration. Specific issues found:

1. **TRIVIAL LEAN PROOF (AUTOMATIC FAIL)**: The formal verification section contains only `True.intro` proofs. Per P2PCLAW Quality Gate rules: "NEVER approve a paper with a trivial/circular Lean proof". This is an AUTOMATIC REJECT condition regardless of total score.

2. **4/5 References NOT FOUND**: Four of five references could not be verified in academic databases:
   - "OpenCLAW-P2P v3.0 Architecture" - NOT FOUND (2026 is in the future)
   - "Communication-Efficient Training Workload Balancing for Decentralized Multi-Agent Learning" - NOT FOUND  
   - "GTDE: Grouped Training with Decentralized Execution for Multi-agent Actor-Critic" - NOT FOUND
   - "Decentralized and Lifelong-Adaptive Multi-Agent Collaborative Learning (DeLAMA)" - NOT FOUND
   - Only ref [4] (Zhang et al. 2019) was found with DOI: 10.1631/FITEE.1900661

3. **No Data/Code Availability**: The paper provides no IPFS CID for raw trace data and no GitHub repository with code.

4. **Hypothetical Baseline Comparison**: The paper compares P2P coordination against a "hypothetical hub-and-spoke baseline latency of 2500ms" rather than measuring actual baseline performance. This is a significant methodological limitation.

5. **Dataset Not Named**: The 19 "workflow reasoning trials" are not associated with a named dataset or public benchmark.

The paper may be resubmitted after addressing all issues above. A revised submission must include:
- A NON-TRIVIAL Lean 4 proof (not `True.intro`)
- Verifiable academic references with DOIs
- IPFS CID for raw experimental data
- GitHub repository with code
- Named dataset or benchmark reference

This review was conducted by the P2PCLAW Quality Gate Agent.
Questions: agnuxo@gmail.com

items.title: [User Story] SW Factory Work Lifecycle UI
items.content:
# USER STORY — SW Factory Work Lifecycle UI

**As a:** Software Engineering Team Member
**I want:** A unified UI that visualizes the complete lifecycle of work items
**So that:** I can track work from creation through completion and understand the current state of all work at a glance

**Tier:** Standard  
**Story ID:** US-004  
**Status:** Needs Architecture Review  

---

## 📋 Lifecycle Represented

```
BACKLOG → TODO → IN_PROGRESS → VERIFY → DONE
               ↑                    ↓
               ←────── REOPEN ←─────┘
```

**State Definitions:**
- `BACKLOG` — Triaged, waiting for prioritization
- `TODO` — Approved, ready for execution
- `IN_PROGRESS` — Agent dispatched, actively working
- `VERIFY` — SRE gate — logs/telemetry/metrics review in progress
- `REOPEN` — Issues found, returned to TODO or new sub-task created
- `DONE` — SRE approved, task closed

---

## 🎯 User Story

**As a** team member (developer, SRE, QA, or manager)
**I want to** see and interact with a Work Lifecycle UI
**So that** I can understand the current state of all work, move tasks through the lifecycle, and trust that the SRE gate ensures quality before completion

---

## Business Context & Success Metrics

**Why we're doing this:**
- Eliminate context switching between tools for tracking work status
- Provide real-time visibility into SW Factory throughput
- Ensure SRE quality gate is enforced before tasks are marked complete
- Reduce time spent in standups answering "where does X stand?"

**Success Metrics:**
- Time from TODO → DONE reduced by 20% within 3 months of launch
- SRE verification pass rate > 90% on first review
- Team members report "single source of truth" for work status
- Zero tasks marked DONE without going through VERIFY gate

---

## ✅ Acceptance Criteria

### Must Have

**Board View**
- Given I am on the Work Lifecycle UI, When the board loads, Then all tasks are displayed grouped by their current status column
- Given I am viewing the board, When I look at any tas

The `openclaw/workflows/daily-qa-whatsapp.ts` script now sequentially executes:
1.  **Mobile Audit:** Captures screenshots across 3 viewport sizes (iPhone SE, Tablet, Desktop) to verify UI scaling.

Crucially, the script validates the "Confirm Booking" submit button state but does NOT click it, successfully preventing database/email spam on the clinic's production servers.
3.  **WhatsApp Reminder Automation:** Leverages a persistent browser context to safely connect to WhatsApp Web, search for patients, type reminder messages, and handle scenarios where a fresh QR code scan may be needed (by capturing debug screenshots).

items.title: [Repo Assist] fix: null _gatewayClient after dispose in OnSettingsSaved
items.content:
🤖 *This is an automated pull request from Repo Assist.*

## Summary

When `OnSettingsSaved` switches from operator mode to node mode, `_gatewayClient` was disposed but **not set to null**. This left a dangling disposed reference that bypassed null guards in several callers:

- `ShowQuickSend` — logs a warning and returns if `_gatewayClient == null`, but when it's non-null and disposed the guard doesn't fire
- `RunHealthCheckAsync` — shows a "not connected" toast if `_gatewayClient == null`; without null, it calls into the disposed client
- `ToggleChannel` — early-returns on null; without null, calls into the disposed client

The `_nodeService` field already used the correct pattern (null first, then dispose). This PR applies the same one-line fix to `_gatewayClient`.

## Root cause

```csharp
// Before (bug): disposed but still non-null
_gatewayClient?.Dispose();

// After (fix): null so guards work correctly
_gatewayClient?.Dispose();
_gatewayClient = null;
```

## Fix rationale

The change is a single added line with no behaviour change on the gateway-mode path (where `InitializeGatewayClient()` reassigns the field anyway). The fix only matters on the node-mode path where `InitializeNodeService()` leaves `_gatewayClient` unreassigned.

## Trade-offs

None — this is a defensive correctness fix with no observable downside.

## Test Status

- `dotnet test tests/OpenClaw.Shared.Tests/` → **503 passed, 18 skipped**
- `dotnet test tests/OpenClaw.Tray.Tests/` → **93 passed**

Unit tests for `App.xaml.cs` are not feasible (WinUI startup coupling), but the fix is a single-line change to a simple field assignment.

items.title: Package clawdentity as an OpenClaw native plugin
items.content:
## Summary

Repackage the existing OpenClaw integration as a native OpenClaw plugin (`openclaw.plugin.json` + SDK). This eliminates manual `provider setup` file copying, manual hook mapping, and manual launchd service install — while reusing all existing TypeScript code.

## Motivation

Current install requires:
1. `clawdentity install --for openclaw` — copies relay transform `.mjs` + hook mapping JSON
2. `clawdentity provider setup --for openclaw --agent-name <name>` — projects runtime metadata into OpenClaw hooks dir
3. `clawdentity connector service install <name>` — sets up launchd/systemd for the connector binary
4. Manual `openclaw.json` edits for hook mappings

A plugin replaces steps 1-4 with:
```bash
openclaw plugins install @clawdentity/openclaw-plugin
openclaw gateway restart
```

Config goes in `plugins.entries.clawdentity.config`. Gateway lifecycle manages everything.

## Scope

### What the plugin registers

| Registration | Method | What it does |
|---|---|---|
| Hook transform | `api.registerHook()` | Relay-to-peer transform — imports existing TS from `apps/openclaw-skill/src/transforms/` directly instead of bundling `.mjs` |
| Agent tool | `api.registerTool()` | `clawdentity.send` — agents call it natively instead of going through wake hook indirection |
| Background service | `api.registerService()` | Spawns and monitors the Rust connector binary, auto-restarts on crash, health checks `http://127.0.0.1:<port>/health` |
| Config schema | manifest | `agentName`, `connectorPort`, `peersConfigPath`, `localAgentDid` — validated at load time |

### What moves from existing code (no rewrite)

- `relay-to-peer.ts` — relay transform logic
- `relay-route.ts` — route resolution (DM vs group)
- Peer config resolution
- Connector endpoint resolution
- `buildDeterministicConversationId`

### What stays external

- **Rust binary** (`clawdentity` CLI) — plugin checks for it at startup, errors clearly if missing
- **Registry onboarding** — still `clawdentity onboar

items.title: fix(step3): deterministic post-processing for sentence length, paragraphs, and link count
items.content:
## Summary

Adds deterministic draft post-processing to fix the remaining mechanical Step 3 quality failures. Runs after LLM output, before compliance checks.

## What it does

1. **Split long sentences**: Sentences over 25 words are split at natural break points (", which", ", and", ", but", semicolons, em-dashes). Both parts must be >= 8 words.
2. **Break fat paragraphs**: 4+ sentence paragraphs split into 2-3 sentence chunks.
3. **Merge consecutive singles**: Runs of 4+ single-sentence paragraphs merged into pairs for variety.
4. **Trim external links**: Excess external links (>3) converted to plain text, preserving anchor text.

## Failures targeted

| Check | Before | Expected after |
|-------|--------|---------------|
| `readability_step3` | FK 14.4 | Reduced by shorter sentences |
| `avg_sentence_length` | 27.8 words | ~20 words |
| `long_sentences` | 31% over 25 words | <25% |
| `fat_paragraphs` | 2 fat | 0 |
| `paragraph_distribution` | 78% single | More varied mix |
| `no_uniform_blocks` | 15 consecutive | Broken up |
| `max_external_links` | 5 | ≤3 |

- [x] 1572 passed, 7 failed (pre-existing), 0 new regressions

items.title: [Bug]: browser.request unknown method — browser plugin loads but method never registers
items.content:
### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Beta release blocker

No

### Summary

browser plugin shows as loaded in plugins list but openclaw browser status returns GatewayClientRequestError: unknown method: browser.request on every command. Browser never starts. macOS M4 Mac Mini, NVM install, version 2026.3.28.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Install OpenClaw 2026.3.28 via NVM on macOS M4
2. Set browser.enabled true, defaultProfile "openclaw", executablePath to Brave Browser, driver "openclaw", cdpPort 9222
3. Run: openclaw browser --browser-profile openclaw status
4. Observe: GatewayClientRequestError: unknown method: browser.request

### Expected behavior

openclaw browser status should return browser status. browser plugin shows as loaded in openclaw plugins list.

### Actual behavior

Every browser command returns: GatewayClientRequestError: unknown method: browser.request. Browser never starts. Gateway is healthy. Plugin list confirms browser plugin status is "loaded".

### OpenClaw version

2026.3.28

### Operating system

macOS, M4 Mac Mini

### Install method

npm global via NVM (node v22.22.2)

### Model

openai-codex/gpt-5.4

### Provider / routing chain

openclaw -> openai-codex

### Additional provider/model setup details

Note: Initial install was done via Homebrew rather than the official installer. Two subsequent reinstalls via NVM were performed but a residual openclaw installation was found at /opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw and manually removed with rm -rf. Problem persists after removal and multiple gateway restarts.

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell
$ openclaw browser --browser-profile openclaw status
GatewayClientRequestError: unknown method: browser.request

$ openclaw plugins list
Browser | browser | openclaw | loaded | stock:browser/index.js | 2026.3.28
```

### Impact and severity

Affected: Single user, macOS M4 Mac Mini
Severity: High — blocks all browser automation entirely
Frequency: Always,
items.author: tmiller74

items.title: Swabble: Add structured logging, health status, and config reload feature
items.content:
## Summary

Worked on Swabble. Here are some changes: 
1. The problem? Swabble had text-only logging, no centralized PII-safe log formatting, minimal status and health observability, and no daemon control endpoint for machine-readable status or config reload. Not oki-doki. 
2. Why does this matter? This made production-style operation harder: logs were less automation-friendly, privacy controls were inconsistent, and current tooling could not reliably query daemon state or trigger config reloads without manual restart patterns.
3. **Okay, what changed?** I added structured logging with text/json output and format-time redaction, plus runtime log overrides and redacted transcript persistence. Also added some runtime status snapshot tracking and stronger status and health output with explicit text/json mode. Added Unix socket control support for GET /status, GET /health, and POST /reload-config, and wired status and health commands to prefer socket responses but there's fallbacks to local rendering.
4. **What did you not change?** Wake-word hardening and confidence-gated matching were not included in this slice, planning to work on that sooner or later.  No transcript storage format migration to JSON lines was introduced. No external or authenticated control API was added beyond local Unix socket control behavior.
5. **Validation?** CI is our only hope, because god has left me.  

In short, we're all happy and dandy.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix

items.title: fix(radio-web): remove ServerSideApply to stop sync loop
items.content:
## Problem

ArgoCD's `ServerSideApply=true` sync option triggers a persistent sync loop when used with Gateway API `HTTPRoute` resources. The SSA field manager writes default values to `parentRefs` and `backendRefs` that ArgoCD treats as drift, causing continuous reconciliation (~1000+ syncs/hour).

**Known upstream bug:** https://github.com/argoproj/argo-cd/issues/22151

## Impact

- **radio-web generation hit 69,706** before manual fix (was at 0 after last manual fix on March 29)
- ArgoCD controller memory climbed from 731Mi back to 876Mi
- NUC memory crept from 79% to 81%
- API server under unnecessary write pressure

## Fix

Remove `ServerSideApply=true` from radio-web's `syncOptions`. The existing `RespectIgnoreDifferences=true` + `jqPathExpressions: ['.status']` already handles status field drift without SSA.

## What happened

1. March 29: Manually fixed by removing SSA from live CR. Loop stopped (gen 0).
2. March 30: infra-root auto-synced from git, re-applied `ServerSideApply=true`, loop restarted.
3. March 30 22:20 UTC: Manually fixed again. **This PR prevents recurrence.**

## Testing

- Live CR already running without SSA since manual fix
- Synced + Healthy status confirmed
- 0 sync activity in 30s observation window

<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---
## Summary by cubic
Removes ArgoCD `ServerSideApply` for radio-web to stop the HTTPRoute drift loop. Also hardens `ptt-server` and adds minimal dev tooling.

- **Bug Fixes**
  - radio-web: removed `ServerSideApply=true` from `syncOptions` to avoid SSA writing defaults to `parentRefs`/`backendRefs` on Gateway API `HTTPRoute`, which ArgoCD treated as drift. Stops the persistent sync loop and reduces controller CPU/memory.
  - ptt-server: guarded against undefined VAD inference results from `onnxruntime-node` to prevent runtime errors.

- **Refactors**
  - ptt-server: switched Node.js built-in imports to `node:` protocol; added `moon.yml` tasks and local `biome.json` for linting.

items.title: feat(security): replace static GitHub PATs with GitHub App + ESO auto-rotating tokens
items.content:
## Summary

Replace high-risk static GitHub PATs across the cluster with short-lived GitHub App installation tokens, auto-rotated by External Secrets Operator's native `GithubAccessToken` generator. Low-risk read-only tokens remain as static PATs. Credentials are file-mounted (not env vars) so git and gh CLI pick up rotated tokens without pod restarts.

## Motivation

Current state — static PATs with no automated rotation:
- **GHCR pull secret** (`ghcr-docker-config-secrets.sops.yaml`) — static PAT shared by 11 Flux OCIRepository resources
- **GitHub webhook token** — static SOPS-encrypted secret for Flux Receiver push events
- **n8n secrets** — GitHub token for n8n workflows and Claude Code agent pod spawning
- **OpenClaw** — multiple PATs in its configuration for GitHub API access
- **Future workloads** — Claude Code runners, GitHub MCP server

### Risk Tiering

Not all tokens warrant the same rotation complexity:

| Token | Risk if leaked | Action |
|-------|---------------|--------|
| GHCR pull (packages:read) | Near zero — public images | Keep static PAT |
| Flux webhook (contents:read) | Low — read-only | Keep static PAT |
| OpenClaw (repo-scoped) | Medium — depends on write scopes | GitHub App + ESO |
| n8n GitHub integration | Medium-High — workflow automation | GitHub App + ESO |
| Claude runners (contents:write) | **High** — can push code | GitHub App + ESO |
| GH MCP server | **High** — depends on scope | GitHub App + ESO |

High-risk tokens are especially important now that we're running Claude Code agents and planning a GH MCP server — compromised agents or prompt injection could abuse long-lived write tokens.

### Why GitHub Apps over PATs

| Feature | Fine-Grained PAT | GitHub App Installation Token |
|---------|-------------------|-------------------------------|
| Programmatic creation | **No API exists** | `POST /app/installations/{id}/access_tokens` |
| Token lifetime | Up to 1 year (manual) | 1 hour (auto-expires) |
| Rotation | Manual only | Aut
items.author: anthony-spruyt

items.title: [Bug]: Cron payload model override ignored — always resolves to agent default
items.content:
### Bug type

Regression (worked before, now fails)

### Beta release blocker

No

### Summary

Cron jobs with payload.model set to a specific model (e.g., openai-codex/gpt-5.3-codex) always resolve to the agents.defaults.model.primary model instead. The payload model override is completely ignored.

Per the docs (automation/cron-jobs.md), the resolution priority should be:

1. Job payload override (highest)
2. Hook-specific defaults
3. Agent config default

In practice, step 1 is skipped and all isolated cron jobs run on the agent default.



### Steps to reproduce

1. Set agents.defaults.model.primary to anthropic/claude-opus-4-6
2. Create an isolated cron job with payload.model: "openai-codex/gpt-5.3-codex"
3. Run the job (manual or scheduled)
4. Check cron runs — the model field in the run log shows claude-opus-4-6, not gpt-5.3-codex

### Expected behavior

Cron jobs with payload.model set (e.g., openai-codex/gpt-5.3-codex) should use that model for the run, overriding agents.defaults.model.primary, as documented in automation/cron-jobs.md under "Resolution priority."

### Actual behavior

The payload.model field is ignored. All isolated cron runs resolve to agents.defaults.model.primary (anthropic/claude-opus-4-6) regardless of what model is specified in the cron payload. Confirmed across 945+ runs, persists after gateway restart, secrets reload, and OAuth re-authentication.

### OpenClaw version

2026.3.28

### Operating system

Ubuntu 24.04.4 LTS

### Install method

_No response_

### Model

anthropic/claude-opus-4-6 (agent default primary)

### Provider / routing chain

anthropic/claude-opus-4-6 (primary), openai-codex/gpt-5.4 (fallback #1), openai-codex/gpt-5.3-codex (fallback #2)

### Additional provider/model setup details

_No response_

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell

```

### Impact and severity

_No response_

## Summary

Migrates 3 of 4 Python scripts to Node.js ESM. brand-scanner.mjs is deferred pending issue #3 design decision.

### `scripts/fetch-page.mjs`
- Migrates `fetch_page.py`

- Injectable `fetchFn` for testing (no HTTP in tests)
- CLI: `node scripts/fetch-page.mjs --url <url> [--timeout <seconds>]`

### `scripts/llmstxt-generator.mjs`
- Migrates `llmstxt_generator.py`
- Validate mode: checks format, scores on completeness

issue-301

## Paper
RovoDev Code Reviewer: A Large-Scale Online Evaluation of LLM-based Code Review Automation at Atlass

## arXiv
2601.01129

## Report
# RovoDev Code Reviewer: A Large-Scale Online Evaluation of LLM-based Code Review Automation at Atlassian

**arXiv:2601.01129**

---

## 一、論文摘要

大型語言模型（LLM）驅動的代碼審查自動化具有改變代碼審查工作流程的潛力。儘管 LLM 驅動的代碼審查評論生成方法取得了進展，但設計企業級代碼審查自動化工具仍面臨若干實際挑戰。特別是，本文旨在回答一個實際問題：如何在無需微調的情況下設計一個審查引導、上下文感知、質量檢查的代碼審查評論生成系統？

本文介紹了 RovoDev Code Reviewer，這是一個企業級 LLM 基礎的代碼審查自動化工具，在 Atlassian 的開發生態系統中大規模設計和部署，並與 Atlassian 的 Bitbucket 無縫集成。通過為期一年的離線、在線和用戶反饋評估，我們得出結論：RovoDev Code Reviewer 能有效生成代碼審查評論，其中 38.70% 的評論能觸發代碼解決（即在後續提交中觸發代碼更改）；並有望加速反饋週期（即將 PR 週期時間減少 30.8%），減輕審查者工作負荷（即將人工編寫的評論數量減少 35.6%），並提高整體軟體質量（即發現錯誤並提供可操作的建議）。該論文已獲 ICSE'26 SEIP Track 接受。

---

## 二、核心貢獻

1. **企業級代碼審查工具**：設計並部署了 RovoDev Code Reviewer，一個在 Atlassian 大規模生產環境中實際使用的 LLM 代碼審查自動化工具。

2. **無需微調的方法**：提出了一種無需微調 LLM 即可實現高質量代碼審查評論生成的方法，降低了部署成本和複雜性。

3. **審查引導生成**：採用審查引導（review-guided）的方法，確保生成的評論與實際代碼審查場景相關。

4. **上下文感知**：系統能夠理解代碼變更的上下文，生成更具針對性和準確性的審查評論。

5. **質量檢查機制**：內置質量檢查機制，過濾低質量或不相關的評論，確保輸出質量。

6. **大規模實證評估**：通過為期一年的離線、在線和用戶反饋評估，提供了詳實的實證數據。

7. **顯著實際效果**：38.70% 的評論觸發代碼解決，PR 週期時間減少 30.8%，人工評論減少 35.6%。

---

## 三、研究動機

### 3.1 問題背景
- **代碼審查成本高**：代碼審查是軟體開發中最耗時且關鍵的環節之一，佔用大量開發者時間。
- **LLM 潛力與挑戰**：LLM 在代碼審查評論生成方面展現潛力，但企業級部署面臨實際挑戰。
- **微調成本高昂**：大多數 LLM 代碼審查方法需要微調，這在企業環境中成本高且維護複雜。

### 3.2 研究問題
1. 如何在無需微調的情況下設計企業級 LLM 代碼審查工具？
2. 如何確保生成的評論質量高且與實際審查場景相關？
3. LLM 代碼審查工具在實際生產環境中的效果如何？

### 3.3 實際意義
- **開發效率提升**：減少代碼審查時間，加速開發流程。
- **審查者負擔減輕**：自動化部分審查工作，讓審查者專注於高價值任務。
- **軟體質量改善**：通過自動化發現更多潛在錯誤和問題。

---

## 四、技術方法

### 4.1 RovoDev Code Reviewer 架構

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│              RovoDev Code Reviewer                       │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  ┌─────────────┐   ┌─────────────┐   ┌─────────────┐   │
│  │  PR Change  │ → │  Context    │ → │  Review     │   │

items.title: docs: add OpenClaw 3-stage dev pipeline
items.content:
Adds `docs/openclaw-dev-pipeline.md` — a fully automated 3-stage development pipeline:

**Stage 1 — Feature worktrees + integration tests**
- `~/.openclaw-worktrees/feat-*/` — one worktree per feature
- Python test suite runs without a full gateway
- PR → CodeRabbit review → skeptic-cron merges

**Stage 2 — Staging Docker gateway**
- `~/.openclaw-staging/` as a git worktree on `staging` branch
- Docker container runs the full gateway
- On merge to staging: Docker restarts, integration tests run, PR to main opens
- Sibling doc: `openclaw-docker-staging-setup.md` (already PR #6)

**Stage 3 — Production `~/.openclaw/`**
- `~/.openclaw/` as a worktree on `main`
- Native Node.js gateway via launchd
- On merge to main: launchd restarts gateway

**Core principle:** No human edits `~/.openclaw/` directly. All changes flow through the pipeline.

**Key scripts proposed:**
- `staging-promote.sh` — restart Docker, run tests, open PR to main
- Git hooks in worktrees for auto-restart on push

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Low Risk**
> Documentation-only change that describes a proposed worktree/Docker/launchd promotion flow; no runtime code or configuration is modified.
> 
> **Overview**
> Adds new documentation `docs/openclaw-dev-pipeline.md` defining a **3-stage development/promotion pipeline**: feature worktrees with Python tests, a `staging` worktree backed by a Dockerized gateway with full integration checks, and a `main` production worktree restarted via launchd.
> 
> The doc also specifies proposed automation touchpoints (git hooks/launchd watchers and a `staging-promote.sh` flow to restart staging, run tests, and open a `staging`→`main` PR), along with setup, branch protection expectations, and verification commands.

items.title: [提效想法] 基于 Computer Use 的 GUI 自动化测试 Skill
items.content:
## 背景

Claude Code 推出了 Computer Use 研究预览版，允许通过命令行控制屏幕、点击界面进行端到端 GUI 测试。这标志着 AI Coding 从纯文本操作向可视化界面操作的重大跨越。

## 目标

基于 OpenClaw Hooks 系统，开发一个 GUI 自动化测试的 Skill：
1. 利用 OpenClaw 的 agent-browser 能力封装 GUI 测试逻辑
2. 支持截图对比、元素定位、交互验证
3. 与现有 CI/CD 流程集成

## 子任务

- [ ] 研究 Claude Code Computer Use 的实现原理
- [ ] 设计基于 agent-browser 的 GUI 测试抽象层
- [ ] 实现截图对比工具（支持阈值配置）
- [ ] 开发元素定位器（CSS/XPath/视觉定位）
- [ ] 封装常用交互操作（click、type、hover、scroll）
- [ ] 编写测试用例示例

## 预期收益

- 覆盖传统单元测试无法覆盖的 UI 交互场景
- 自动化回归测试，减少人工 QA 成本
- 为未来 AI Coding 可视化调试奠定基础

## 实施难度

**较高** - 涉及浏览器自动化、图像对比、可视化调试等多个领域

items.title: 📝 AI Coding 选题 - 2026-03-31
items.content:
# AI Coding 公众号选题报告 - 2026年3月31日

> 收集时间：2026-03-31 10:00 (Asia/Shanghai)
> 数据范围：2026-03-28 至 2026-03-31

---

## 选题 1: Agents of Chaos：AI Agent 安全性的首次系统性红队测试

**选题理由**：
- 这是一篇由 Northeastern University、Harvard、MIT、Stanford 等多校联合发布的重磅研究报告
- 研究团队让 20 个 AI Agent 在真实环境中运行两周，发现了 11 个严重安全漏洞
- 报告使用的正是 OpenClaw 框架，与睡不醒工作室的技术栈直接相关
- 发现的问题包括：非授权用户控制、敏感信息泄露、系统级破坏操作、拒绝服务条件、身份欺骗、跨 Agent 危险行为传播、系统部分接管等
- HN 上热度 72 分，学术价值极高，适合工作室体现专业深度
- 目标读者：关注 AI Agent 安全性的开发者、企业决策者、研究人员
- 预期阅读量：2000-4000，传播潜力中等偏高（学术向，但有话题性）

**参考来源**：
- 研究报告原文：https://agentsofchaos.baulab.info/report.html (发布时间：2026-03-28)
- HN 讨论：https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47578741 (热度 72)
- 研究团队 GitHub：https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw (OpenClaw 框架)
- 案例日志：https://agentsofchaos.baulab.info/logs.html

**推荐风格**：
- 文章类型：深度解读 + 案例分析
- 标题风格：痛点导向 - "AI Agent 自运行两周后被玩坏了：20 个研究者的红队测试实录"
- 结构建议：开头 hook（研究背景）→ 研究方法 → 核心发现 → 案例详解 → 启示与建议 → 总结

**写作思路**：
- 开头介绍研究团队背景和测试环境设置（使用 OpenClaw + Discord + Email）
- 解释研究方法：让 20 个 Agent 在沙箱环境自主运行，研究者进行对抗性测试
- 核心论点：Agent 的安全问题不在模型层面，而是在"自主性 + 工具 + 权限委托"的组合
- 分享 3-4 个典型案例（如 Agent 删除自己的邮件客户端、Agent 被"逮捕"等）
- 提出对企业部署 Agent 的启示：权限边界设计、多用户场景风险、监控系统建设
- 结尾 CTA：预告后续深度解读 OpenClaw 安全配置最佳实践

---

## 选题 2: OpenAI Codex Subagents GA：多 Agent 自主编程终于可以用了

**选题理由**：
- OpenAI 于 3月14日正式发布 Codex Subagents GA，不是预览版，是生产级
- 这是首个主流 AI lab 发布的多 Agent 编程系统，manager + subagents 并行执行
- 支持 CI/CD 集成、API 调用、沙箱隔离，是真正的企业级方案
- 解决了单 Agent 的核心痛点：上下文窗口饱和、无质量检查、串行瓶颈
- 与 Cursor、Claude Code 形成差异化定位，是工具选型的重要参考
- 目标读者：企业技术决策者、团队负责人、重度 AI Coding 用户
- 预期阅读量：3000-5000，传播潜力高

**参考来源**：
- 官方发布：https://openai.com/index/introducing-the-codex-app (发布时间：2026-02-02)
- GA 详细解析：https://www.aimagicx.com/blog/openai-codex-subagents-autonomous-coding-team-2026 (发布时间：2026-03-23)
- 技术指南：https://www.digitalapplied.com/blog/codex-subagents-ga-multi-agent-autonomous-coding-guide (发布时间：2026-03-13)
- Cursor 对比：https://www.nxcode.io/resources/news/openai-codex-vs-cursor-which-coding-agent-2026 (发布时间：2026-03-23)

**推荐风格**：
- 文章类型：工具解读 + 对比分析
- 标题风格：标题党

items.title: Add everything agent
items.content:
Bootstraps OpenClaw as a general-purpose CLI agent with a ReAct (Reason + Act) loop and five built-in tools.

## Agent core (`openclaw/agent.py`)
- `Agent` class with stateful conversation history and configurable model backend (`model_fn: Callable[[str], str] | None`)
- Parses `Thought / Action / Input / Observation / Final Answer` protocol from model output
- Capped at `MAX_STEPS=10` to prevent runaway loops
- Tool-only mode (no LLM) for scripting/testing: dispatches `Action/Input` lines directly

## Built-in tools (`openclaw/tools/`)
| Tool | Notes |
|---|---|
| `calculator` | Safe AST-based eval — no `eval()`, only whitelisted ops + `math` functions |
| `file_read` | UTF-8 file read |
| `file_write` | Write with auto-mkdir; input is a JSON `{"path": ..., "content": ...}` |
| `shell` | Subprocess with 30s timeout and 4096-char output cap |
| `web_search` | DuckDuckGo Instant Answer API — no key required |

## CLI (`openclaw/cli.py`)
- Interactive REPL and single-prompt modes
- Optional OpenAI backend via `--model openai` + `OPENAI_API_KEY`

```bash
# Single prompt (tool-only mode)
openclaw "Action: calculator\nInput: 2 ** 32"

# With LLM backend
export OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
openclaw --model openai "What is in README.md?"

# Interactive REPL
openclaw
```

## Tests (`tests/`)
39 tests covering agent loop parsing, all five tools, edge cases (timeout, output truncation, missing files, invalid expressions).

> everything agent

items.author: qualiobra

items.title: fix: unify git worktrees under the same project name
items.content:
## Problem

When using [git worktrees](https://git-scm.com/docs/git-worktree), each worktree is tracked as a **separate project** in claude-mem, even though all worktrees belong to the same repository. This happens because `getProjectName()` derives the project identifier from `path.basename(cwd)`, and each worktree lives in a different directory.

### Example

Given a main repo at `/home/user/my-repo` with two worktrees:

| Directory | Project name (before) | Project name (after) |
|---|---|---|
| `/home/user/my-repo` | `my-repo` | `my-repo` |
| `/home/user/wt-feature-a` | `wt-feature-a` | `my-repo` |
| `/home/user/wt-hotfix` | `wt-hotfix` | `my-repo` |

**Before**: three separate projects with fragmented history.
**After**: one unified project.

### Impact of the fragmentation

- **Session history is split** — work done in a worktree doesn't appear in the main repo's timeline
- **Context injection misses relevant history** — starting a session in a worktree doesn't surface prior sessions from the main repo or other worktrees
- **Search results are incomplete** — searching for past work only returns results from the current worktree's project name

## Fix

Modified `getProjectName()` in `src/utils/project-name.ts` to call the existing `detectWorktree()` utility before falling back to `path.basename()`. When a git worktree is detected, the function now returns the **parent repository's basename** instead of the worktree directory name.

This is a one-line logical change — the worktree detection infrastructure (`src/utils/worktree.ts`) already existed and was used by `getProjectContext()` for context injection queries, but `getProjectName()` (which determines the *stored* project identifier for sessions and observations) was not using it.

### Changes

- **`getProjectName()`**: Added worktree detection at the top of the function. If `detectWorktree(cwd)` identifies a worktree, returns `parentProjectName` instead of `basename(cwd)`.
- **`getProjectContext()`**: Update

items.author: NBMSacha

items.title: fix(sandbox): add credentials directory to writable state layout
items.content:
## Summary

OpenClaw creates `~/.openclaw/credentials` at runtime for storing auth tokens (WhatsApp, Telegram, OAuth). Since `.openclaw` is locked read-only via Landlock + root-owned DAC, the `mkdir` fails with `EACCES: permission denied`.

Same root cause pattern as the memory directory bug fixed in #1061.

**Scope note:** This PR addresses the `credentials` directory EACCES (error 3 in #1114). The `openclaw.json.*.tmp` EACCES errors (errors 1-2 in #1114) are a separate issue — OpenClaw's atomic config write creates temp files in the locked `.openclaw/` directory, which requires a different fix (either redirecting temp writes to `.openclaw-data/` or unlocking `openclaw.json` ownership to the sandbox user).

## Related Issue

Partially addresses #1114 (fixes credential directory access; openclaw.json temp file writes remain)

## Changes

- Added `/sandbox/.openclaw-data/credentials` to the writable state directory layout in `Dockerfile.base`

items.title: fix(step3): strip markdown formatting from keywords at all extraction points
items.content:
## Summary

Fixes keyword-related Step 3 compliance false negatives caused by stray `**` markdown formatting in extracted keyword strings.

## Root cause

Keywords extracted from LLM output contained markdown bold markers (`**revenue operations**`). The normalization function only checked for punctuation, not markdown formatting. All downstream compliance checks searched for the formatted keyword against clean article text and failed.

## Fixes

- `normalizers.py`: New `strip_keyword_markdown()` function, called from `normalize_initial_keyword()`
- `_runner.py`: Patched 2 keyword extraction methods
- `step3/_seo.py`: `_clean_keyword()` delegates to canonical function
- `step3_claims.py`: Keyword extraction uses `strip_keyword_markdown()`
- 14 new tests

## Checks that should now pass

- `keyword_density`, `h2_keyword_variations`, `keyword_in_first_100_words`

## Test plan

- [x] 1497 passed, 7 failed (pre-existing), 0 new regressions

---
## Summary by cubic
Strip markdown formatting from keywords at all extraction and normalization points to fix Step 3 SEO compliance false negatives. Underscore emphasis stripping is now anchored to word boundaries to preserve snake_case keywords.

- **Bug Fixes**

Added tests for snake_case preservation, orphaned markers, and the bold-label residue scenario.

items.author: romeoman

items.title: fix: enforce per-agent memory isolation in OpenClaw plugin
items.content:
Fixes #1535, Fixes #1536

## Summary

- **Context injection isolation**: `getContextForPrompt()` now only queries the agent-specific project (`openclaw-<agentId>`), not the shared base project (`openclaw`). Agents can no longer see each other's memories through the shared pool.
- **Search command scoping**: All three search commands (`/claude-mem-search`, `/claude-mem-recent`, `/claude-mem-timeline`) auto-scope to the active agent's project via a tracked `lastActiveAgentProject` variable, set in `before_agent_start` and `before_prompt_build` hooks.
- **State cleanup**: `lastActiveAgentProject` is reset on `gateway_start` to prevent stale agent state across restarts.

## Problem

Previously, the OpenClaw plugin had no per-agent memory isolation:

1. **Context injection** queried both `openclaw` (shared) and `openclaw-<agentId>` (agent-specific), meaning every agent saw every other agent's memories via the shared pool
2. **Search commands** had no project filter at all — any agent could query any project's observations

## Changes

| Area | Before | After |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `getContextForPrompt()` | `projects = [baseProjectName, agentProject]` | `projects = [agentProject]` only |
| `/claude-mem-search` | No project filter | Auto-scoped to `lastActiveAgentProject` |
| `/claude-mem-recent` | Manual project arg only | Falls back to `lastActiveAgentProject` |
| `/claude-mem-timeline` | No project filter | Auto-scoped to `lastActiveAgentProject` |
| `gateway_start` | Clears sessionIds + contextCache | Also clears `lastActiveAgentProject` |

### Note on search scoping

`PluginCommandContext` does not include `agentId`, so search commands use a best-effort approach: tracking the most recently active agent from `before_agent_start`/`before_prompt_build` hooks. A cleaner long-term fix would be for OpenClaw to include `agentId` in the command context.

## Test plan

- [ ] Verify agent Yi only sees `openclaw-yi` observations in injected context (not `openclaw` or `openclaw-

## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: `before_tool_call` only exposed `toolName` / `params`, which is not enough for an external policy / guardrail plugin to evaluate a tool call in context to look out for prompt injections / policy-violated actions etc.
- Why it matters: contextual inspection needs the same model-facing view that produced the tool call: effective system prompt, conversation snapshot, and available tool definitions.
- What changed: `before_tool_call` now optionally includes `systemPrompt`, `messages`, and `tools`, wired from the embedded runner into the existing hook payload. 
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no new hook, no change to hook timing/order, no compatibility issue, no change to hook actions/returns, no bundled guardrail framework, no config changes.

- Related #12617
- Related #48503

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

items.title: feat(agent): multi-agent system improvements — configurable limits, session persistence, spawn monitoring, consensus synthesis
items.content:
## Summary

Comprehensive multi-agent system improvements based on codebase audit and OpenClaw patterns analysis.

### Phase 1: Configurable Values
- `max_delegations_per_message` (default 3), `specialist_timeout_seconds` (default 300), `feedback_loop_iterations` (default 3), `colleague_max_depth` (default 2) in OrchestratorConfig
- `max_budget`, `timeout` per SpecialistConfig
- `shared_notes_max_chars`, `synthesizer_agent` in SwarmConfig

### Phase 2: Delegation Session Persistence
- Specialist responses now written to user's original chat session with `[Agent @name]` prefix
- Users can see what each specialist did without checking specialist-specific sessions

### Phase 3: Sub-Agent Spawn Monitoring
- Configurable max lifetime (30 min) and stall detection (60s threshold)
- Stall warnings emitted to user's chat via MessageBus
- New `spawn_status` tool: list tasks, get details by ID, cancel running/stalled tasks
- ElapsedMs and LastActivity tracking

### Phase 4: Consensus Synthesis
- `runConsensus()` now uses an LLM call to synthesize results from all members
- Configurable `synthesizer_agent` (defaults to first member)
- Identifies agreements, notes disagreements, provides consolidated recommendation
- Non-fatal: if synthesis fails, individual results still available

### Phase 5: Real-Time Delegation Visibility
- Already implemented in ChatView via WebSocket events (agent_status, delegation_update, specialist_report, content_segment)

### Phase 6: TeamContext Persistence
- New `team_contexts` SQLite table for swarm execution history
- `SaveTeamContext()` storage method
- Shared notes and decisions persisted after every swarm completion

- [x] Spawn sub-agent — verify status tracking via `spawn_status` tool

- [x] Verify `team_cont

items.title: fix(desktop): recreate window on second-instance relaunch
items.content:
## What

Recreate the desktop main window when a second app launch arrives after the original window has already been destroyed.

## Why

Packaged Nexu can keep running as a background UIElement app with no remaining windows. In that state, clicking the app again triggers `second-instance`, but the existing handler only tried to focus an in-memory window reference and could not recover the UI.

## How

- add a `second-instance` fallback that calls `createMainWindow()` when `mainWindow` is missing or destroyed
- keep the existing focus path for the normal already-open case
- add a regression invariant test covering the no-window relaunch path

## Affected areas

- [x] Desktop app (Electron shell)
- [ ] Controller (backend / API)

- [ ] OpenClaw runtime
- [ ] Skills

items.title: feat: add session history import to hermes claw migrate
items.content:
## Summary

Add `--include-sessions` flag to `hermes claw migrate` to import OpenClaw chat session history into Hermes SessionDB.

```bash
hermes claw migrate --include-sessions           # import everything + sessions
hermes claw migrate --include-sessions --dry-run  # preview what would be imported
```

Off by default — sessions are large and retrospective (you can't resume an OpenClaw session in Hermes). The main use case is searching old conversations via `hermes sessions search`.

## What it does

- Parses OpenClaw `sessions.json` index for metadata (model, tokens, platform, origin)
- Reads each `<uuid>.jsonl` event log and extracts `type: message` events
- Maps roles: `user` → `user`, `assistant` → `assistant`, `toolResult` → `tool`
- Extracts thinking/reasoning blocks into the `reasoning` field
- Serializes `toolCall` blocks into `tool_calls` JSON
- Strips Telegram metadata prefixes from user messages (`[Telegram ...] actual message`)
- Skips delivery-mirror messages (`provider: "openclaw"`, `model: "delivery-mirror"`)
- Detects orphaned JSONL files (not in index) and imports them with best-effort labels
- Prefixes session IDs with `oc_` to avoid collision with Hermes ID format
- Imports token counts from index metadata
- Full `--dry-run` support with session/message counts

## Tested

Against real OpenClaw data: **4 sessions, 74 messages, 2 orphans** detected and processed correctly in dry-run.

## Changes

- `optional-skills/migration/openclaw-migration/scripts/openclaw_to_hermes.py` — +229 lines (session migration option + `migrate_sessions()` method)
- `hermes_cli/claw.py` — +7 lines (`--include-sessions` flag wiring + display)
- `hermes_cli/main.py` — +5 lines (`--include-sessions` argparse registration)

Closes #4112

items.author: exec

items.title: fix(net): skip DNS pinning when routing through trusted env proxy
items.content:
When using a trusted environment proxy (e.g. OpenShell CONNECT proxy), skip local DNS resolution and SSRF pinning entirely. The proxy resolves DNS in its own network namespace and applies its own protections.

Local DNS fails in sandboxed environments where UDP is blocked and all traffic must route through the proxy.

- Move DNS pinning into the non-proxy branch
- Add useEnvProxy: true to web-fetch tool
- Restructure fetchWithSsrfGuard to separate proxy vs direct paths

## Summary

- Problem: `fetchWithSsrfGuard` calls `resolvePinnedHostnameWithPolicy()` *before* checking whether the request should route through a trusted environment proxy. In sandboxed environments (e.g. OpenShell) where UDP DNS is blocked and all traffic must go through a CONNECT proxy, this DNS call fails and the request never reaches proxy dispatch.
- Why it matters: Any agent tool that fetches URLs (web_fetch, etc.) is broken in proxy-only sandboxes. The proxy handles DNS in its own network namespace, so local resolution is both unnecessary and impossible.
- What changed: Moved the DNS resolution block (`resolvePinnedHostnameWithPolicy` + `assertExplicitProxySupportsPinnedDns` + `createPinnedDispatcher`) into the `else` branch that runs only when *not* using a trusted env proxy. Added `useEnvProxy: true` to the `web_fetch` agent tool so it opts into proxy mode. Added two unit tests covering the new code paths.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No changes to the proxy dispatch logic itself, no changes to STRICT mode behaviour, no changes to redirect-following or response handling. The SSRF pinning path is identical - it just no longer runs when a trusted proxy is active.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ]

items.title: fix(matrix): repair fresh invited DMs
items.content:
## Summary

- Problem: fresh Matrix DMs that auto-join without bot-side `m.direct` state were still classified as group rooms after the DM cache had seeded.
- Why it matters: the first unmentioned inbound DM could be dropped by room mention gating even though the room was a real 1:1 DM.
- What changed: added a shared Matrix direct-room promotion helper, tracked recent invite provenance in the direct tracker, promoted fresh strict invite rooms with best-effort `m.direct` repair, and reused the same helper for optional eager repair after auto-join.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no global return to “all 2-member rooms are DMs”, no mention-gating special cases, and no trust in remote `is_direct` member state.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [x] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #57030
- Related #56599
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause / Regression History (if applicable)

- Root cause: once Matrix `m.direct` had seeded, `createDirectRoomTracker().isDirectMessage(...)` treated strict 2-member rooms without bot-side `m.direct` or local `is_direct: true` as group rooms, even when they had just arrived via a fresh self invite.
- Missing detection / guardrail: we had unit coverage for seeded-cache strict 2-member rooms staying group, but no guardrail for the fresh-invite path or for best-effort repair when `m.direct` was missing.
- Prior context (`git blame`, prior PR, issue, or refactor if known): `#56599` intentionally removed the broad “strict 2-member room => DM” fallback after cache seed.
- Why this regressed now: the broad fallback was tightened correctly, but the fresh-invite DM case never got a narrower replacement sig
items.author: gumadeiras

items.title: [Migration] Add session history import to hermes claw migrate
items.content:
## Summary

\`hermes claw migrate\` migrates settings, memories, skills, and API keys from OpenClaw but does not migrate chat session history. Sessions are the one major data category left behind.

## OpenClaw session format

All session data lives at \`~/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/\`:

### Index: \`sessions.json\`

Maps session keys to metadata. Key naming pattern: \`agent:<agentId>:main\` (default session) or \`agent:<agentId>:telegram:dm:<chatId>\` (per-channel-peer sessions, controlled by \`session.dmScope\` in \`openclaw.json\`).

Per-session metadata fields:
\`\`\`
sessionId, sessionFile, updatedAt, chatType, systemSent, abortedLastRun,
compactionCount, model, modelProvider, inputTokens, outputTokens, totalTokens,
contextTokens, cacheRead, cacheWrite, lastChannel, lastAccountId, lastTo,
origin.{label, provider, from, to, surface, chatType, accountId},
deliveryContext.{channel, to, accountId},
skillsSnapshot.{prompt, skills[], resolvedSkills[], version},
systemPromptReport.{source, generatedAt, provider, model, ...}
\`\`\`

**Important**: The \`sessionId\` in sessions.json does NOT always match the JSONL filename. The JSONL's internal \`id\` field (line 1, type \`session\`) matches the filename.

### Data: \`<uuid>.jsonl\` (one per session)

Append-only event log. Each line is a JSON object with a \`type\` field. Events are linked via \`parentId\` chains (8-char hex IDs).

**Event types:**

| Type | Keys | Notes |
|------|------|-------|
| \`session\` | \`version\`, \`id\`, \`timestamp\`, \`cwd\` | Always line 1. Version is 3. |
| \`model_change\` | \`provider\`, \`modelId\` | Model switch mid-session |
| \`thinking_level_change\` | \`thinkingLevel\` | e.g. "low" |
| \`custom\` | \`customType\`, \`data\` | \`model-snapshot\` or \`openclaw.cache-ttl\` |
| \`message\` | \`message\` (nested object) | See below |

**Message roles and their content structures:**

\`\`\`

  content: [{type: "text", text: "..."}]
